item.title: Update: New World Screwworm detected in Texas, New Mexico; California remains clear
item.description:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has confirmed that the New World Screwworm – an invasive, destructive parasite – has been detected in Texas and New Mexico, marking the the pest’s first incursion into the United States since it was successfully eradicated in the … Continue reading →

item.title: How to Grow and Care For Lewisia Bitterroot (Cliff Maids)
item.description:
Lewisia, also called bitterroot or cliff maids, is a small succulent native to the rocky mountains and outcroppings of western North America. It forms a rosette of thick, fleshy leaves topped with bright flowers in pink, yellow, orange, purple, and white in spring and early summer. Learn how to grow lewisia in this guide.

The post How to Grow and Care For Lewisia Bitterroot (Cliff Maids) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Lewisia, also called bitterroot or cliff maids, is a small succulent native to the rocky mountains and outcroppings of western North America. It forms a rosette of thick, fleshy leaves topped with bright flowers in pink, yellow, orange, purple, and white in spring and early summer. Learn how to grow lewisia in this guide.
The post [How to Grow and Care For Lewisia Bitterroot (Cliff Maids)](https://gardenerspath.com/plants/succulents/grow-lewisia/) appeared first on [Gardener's Path](https://gardenerspath.com).

item.title: How to Plant and Grow Aronia (Chokeberry)
item.description:
Aronia, aka chokeberry, is a native North American shrub hardy to Zone 3. It produces berries high in antioxidants, with white spring flowers and brilliant red fall foliage. The berries are astringent when fresh but excellent for jam, juice, wine, and tinctures. Learn how to grow aronia in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Plant and Grow Aronia (Chokeberry) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for New Zealand Flax
item.description:
With long, sword-like and richly colored leaves, New Zealand flax develops into large, vibrant clusters of bold structural growth for both sun and shade gardens. item.title: Center for Analytical Chemistry staff gain better appreciation of lab work through sampling ride-along
item.description:
CDFA Center for Analytical Chemistry (CAC) Food Safety Laboratories provide analytical services to the Department of Pesticide Regulation (DPR) through analyzing various samples DPR staff take from the field. To observe firsthand the sampling techniques utilized by DPR, Food Safety Laboratory … Continue reading →

The post Center for Analytical Chemistry staff gain better appreciation of lab work through sampling ride-along appeared first on CDFA's Planting Seeds Blog.
item.source:markdown:
CDFA Center for Analytical Chemistry (CAC) Food Safety Laboratories provide analytical services to the Department of Pesticide Regulation (DPR) through analyzing various samples DPR staff take from the field.
